creator: alex@dawnmail.net
text:
(In reply to J.R. Oldroyd from comment #5)
It did not work in FreeBSD 14.1 either. But, I did not troubleshoot it.
I installed FreeBSD 14.2 in the hope that it would be fixed. It didn't.

Once again, deleting /usr/local/etc/sane.d/dll.d/epsonscan2 is a *workaround*
for me. It may not be the fix. The segmentation fault is generated by
sane-backends-1.3.1/backend/dll.c. Getting segmentation fault because of
/usr/local/etc/sane.d/dll.d/epsonscan2 is too harsh, I think.
Sorry, I did not dig deeper into sane-backends-1.3.1.

Also, my Epson printer is connected to my computer through an ethernet cable.
It's not a USB scanner.
